_doc: Editorial config for the data tables on /benchmarks/. The engine-comparison table pulls its numeric values from scalability_results.json (the raw bench output); this file supplies display labels, captions, and the scenario picker's commentary. Speed-cell templates: {throughput}, {mp}, {memory} are substituted from the matching engine's row at the showcase MP. Each table may carry an optional column_sizing block to override the default first-column auto-sizing rule (see _column_sizing_doc below).
_column_sizing_doc: Default first-column auto-sizing rule used by both tables. The renderer measures each first-column cell at single-line max-content width; if that width exceeds threshold_ratio × the resulting other-column width, the first column is capped at cap_ratio × the other-column width and its cells wrap. Override per-table by placing a `column_sizing` block inside engine_table or scenario_table.

engine_table._showcase_mp_target_doc: Renders the bench row whose megapixel count is closest to this target. Anchors the showcase numbers to the size the surrounding prose discusses, so the table doesn't drift onto a larger benchmarked size whenever the bench grid grows. Update both this value and the prose in benchmarks.html together if you want a different size.

scenario_table.caption: Choosing an engine for your scenario

scenarios.best_when: RAM is abundant and you want raw throughput.

scenarios.speed_template: Good — {throughput} tiles/s in a {memory} MB envelope
scenarios.best_when: Tight memory ceiling (e.g. 512 MB pod) on potentially huge images.
Spare cores you can spend on parallel strip rendering.
Workload spans many image sizes; default for EngineKind::Auto.
